hard.next_step: 6/16 - Amanda Moir who reports directly to new CPO Helen Whitehouse is scheduled to attend June 25th Toronto event. Have EM'd and WhatsApp'd Ludovic with no response to date. Inviting Amanda and Helen to Horizon in Sept. 4/25MF - no response to our reach outs. Invited a number of McCain procurement titles to come to our booth at Gartner May 4-6 in Orlando. Working to get a live call set with Hackett Group and inviting to Horizon in September. 03/10mc- McCain remains active, but timing is being shaped by recent organizational restructuring and leadership transition. Spoke on the phone with the new CPO, who asked that we reconnect at the end of March. Sending invite for March 30th to reconnect with her. Ludovic has been out of office and is expected back by end of this week, at which point we will confirm continuity, revalidate what remains in scope from the analytics initiative, and assess how much sponsorship carries forward. Strategy is to keep both threads warm and aligned so we preserve the work already done with Ludovic while creating an opening with the new CPO; key risk is disruption or reprioritization from the internal changes.
hard.ais_why:
• Champion Ludovic Verdelet RSVP'd to the Vienna Zycus AI procurement event and asked about Heineken's AI-focused session, including how AI shows up in iNegotiate and iContract. That's curiosity about an AI-themed event, not a buyer-stated need for AI in the McCain deal.
• The most recent 2/13/2026 Avoma call was housekeeping and event coordination, with no Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA, or Intake mentioned by McCain. Earlier discovery shows Merlin and Agentic AI in scope and a mid-2025 POC on taxonomy and automation, but that work paused when the CPO transitioned and AI dialogue has gone quiet since.
• Ludovic also flagged that the team is still waiting for clarity on McCain's coming reorganization, with Ellen Whitehouse newly in the CPO seat and no roadmap shared. There is no champion bandwidth for an AI POC right now, and the only recent engagement was event logistics, which keeps this at the cautious end of AI Curious.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal frozen at the CPO-approval gate for about 8 months; champion Ludovic Verdelet non-responsive to recent email and WhatsApp, but Amanda Moir engaged well on Agentic AI at the 25 Jun 2026 Toronto event, opening a fresh thread.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 1 Aug 2025, about 11 months old. In Shortlisted with close 26 Feb 2027 (about 235 days out from 6 Jul 2026). Last Salesforce activity 26 Jun 2026 is rep-logged from the Toronto event; the last recorded buyer CALL was 13 Feb 2026 (about 4.8 months ago) and it was housekeeping only. The POC has sat on hold since 24 Oct 2025 and missed every restart target (Nov 2025 CPO approval, Jan 2026 POC start, mid-Feb 2026 org clarity, end-Mar 2026 CPO reconnect). A Feb 2027 close remains reachable if the incoming CPO unblocks soon, but there is no forcing function and the champion has gone quiet.
evidence: POC placed on hold pending incoming CPO approval (Ludovic Verdelet, 24 Oct 2025 call): 'I proposed and it has been accepted to put things on hold. I don't want you, me, and our teams to invest time in something that should deliver nothing.'
Org still undefined as of 13 Feb 2026 (Ludovic Verdelet): 'I don't know what will be the organization, who will be in, who will be out' and 'will I decide to be part of it or not?'
SF Next_Step__c (16 Jun 2026): rep has emailed and WhatsApp'd Ludovic 'with no response to date.'

SF Task (26 Jun 2026): 'Amanda Moir attended Toronto RT event and had good questions around AI deployment and AgenticAI use cases, supporting Intake/Orchestration and ANA as 2 areas which can drive immediate impact.'

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI has been put to the buyer. The only value signal is qualitative urgency from Ludovic Verdelet on 10 Dec 2025, that 'every month left behind us without moving is a month lost' and that lost time 'is something we'll have to recover.' No cost, savings, cycle-time, headcount or error-rate figure has been attached to the McCain analytics initiative in any call or field. Quantifying the cost of the roughly 8-month delay and the savings a scoped iAnalyze/Intake POC would unlock is the move that gives incoming Helen Whitehouse a reason to prioritize this over inertia.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified by name and title but has never been engaged by Zycus. Helen Whitehouse (Chief Procurement Officer, an internal promotion from the APAC VP seat) is the new CPO who must re-approve the analytics strategy before the POC restarts, per Ludovic on 24 Oct 2025: 'I'd rather have this strategy approved by Ellen right now because she will be the one for the years to come.' Salesforce Next_Step__c (16 Jun 2026) confirms she owns approval and that Zycus is trying to reach her through a September Horizon invite. She has never had a direct working conversation with Zycus, so commercial appetite and view of the analytics strategy are unknown; securing a direct CPO-office alignment session is the gating move.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are upstream Source-to-Contract capability, not procure-to-pay: on 10 Dec 2025 the parties agreed to focus the 'first look' on intake, tail/tactical spend and the ANA analytics work and explicitly to 'stay away from p to p' ('it's out of scope'). Ludovic also framed a market-comparison criterion, that McCain must understand 'the different offers on the market and the functionalities of each tool.' Amanda Moir's 25 Jun 2026 questions sharpened the criterion toward Agentic AI and Intake/Orchestration and ANA as 'immediate impact' areas (SF Task 26 Jun 2026). Criteria are directional and champion-stated, not yet validated by the incoming CPO or a formal RFP.

decision_process.narrative: The process is clear in shape but stalled on approval. Ludovic confirmed on 24 Oct 2025 the decision stays 'procurement ownership, procurement teams, procurement management, and general management if needed,' with a newly forming procurement Center of Excellence (COE) to own analytics (10 Dec 2025). The gate is incoming Helen Whitehouse re-approving the analytics strategy after she finalizes the reorg and takes it to the board, originally targeted mid/end Feb 2026 but still unresolved as of the 16 Jun 2026 Next Step. Ludovic's VP (Maxim) is withholding org detail even from Ludovic (13 Feb 2026), so the approval chain above procurement is opaque. The buyer's own next step is CPO sign-off, which unblocks the POC.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics begin and end at an unexecuted NDA. On 24 Oct 2025 Ludovic agreed to move on the NDA immediately ('it makes sense for me to go on the NDA right now, this is just a way to protect the company') and committed to share the Zycus template with McCain legal after his PTO. No later call or Next Step evidences the NDA being signed, and no MSA/SOW, procurement channel, InfoSec review, or partner/SI scoping has surfaced. Until the NDA is executed, POC data-sharing and commercials stay blocked; confirming NDA status is the cheapest unblock and would create visibility into McCain's paper process.

sources: deal record (original POC scope: taxonomy accuracy + automation)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is real but qualitative. McCain wants to advance a board-approved analytics strategy and modernize upstream procurement (intake, tail/tactical spend, taxonomy accuracy and automation per the original POC scope), and Ludovic repeatedly framed the delay itself as pain, 'every month left behind us without moving is a month lost' (10 Dec 2025). The deeper confirmed pain is organizational disruption blocking a strategy Ludovic personally champions; the pain owner is procurement (Ludovic, incoming Helen Whitehouse and the forming COE). The gap is that the operational or financial cost of today's manual/in-house approach has never been articulated in numbers.

champion.narrative: Ludovic Verdelet is the sole champion, genuinely engaged but personally at risk. He carried the relationship across every recorded call, advocated internally ('our proposal to Helene, let's jump on the POC, we still believe this is something where we have to go', 24 Oct 2025), agreed to the NDA and a late-January enhancements preview, and transparently invited Zycus into the reorg. The risk is twofold: he is single-threaded (he is the only buyer contact role on this opp) and his own future is uncertain ('will I decide to be part of it or not?', 13 Feb 2026), with his VP withholding org detail from him. He has also gone non-responsive to recent email and WhatsApp (Next Step, 16 Jun 2026). Amanda Moir's 25 Jun 2026 event engagement is the first sign of a second thread; developing her is now urgent. 'Developing', not 'strong', because his advocacy is real but he can no longer move the deal alone and has gone quiet.

sources: Avoma call 10 Dec 2025 (Ludovic Verdelet, Alexa Bradley)
competition.narrative: The dominant competitor is Do Nothing / organizational inertia: the project is frozen behind the McCain reorg, not behind a rival vendor, and on 24 Oct 2025 Ludovic himself chose to pause it. He acknowledged on 10 Dec 2025 that McCain must understand 'the different offers on the market and the functionalities of each tool,' and Alexa Bradley noted the same day 'it may not be just us, it might be 6 other suppliers,' so a multi-vendor field is anticipated but unnamed in current evidence. Coupa, SpendHQ and an in-house/manual approach are carried on record from prior sweeps with no fresh mention this run, so they hold as historical context, not fresh threats. The wedge is the Merlin/Agentic AI and analytics story (intake, tail spend, ANA) that Amanda Moir responded to on 25 Jun 2026, used to differentiate before a formal bake-off forms.

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is rep-driven, not buyer-driven: the last genuine buyer call was 13 Feb 2026 (housekeeping), and everything since is rep outreach or event logistics. The one bright spot is Amanda Moir's 25 Jun 2026 Toronto-event engagement. The owner's manager Alexa Bradley is already engaged (she was on the 10 Dec 2025 call), so executive cover already exists.

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is Do Nothing / organizational inertia: an approximately 8-month internal reorg has frozen the deal and the buyer paused it himself on 24 Oct 2025. A formal multi-vendor evaluation is anticipated ('it might be 6 other suppliers', Alexa Bradley, 10 Dec 2025) but no rival is named in current evidence. Coupa, SpendHQ and an in-house/manual approach remain on record from prior sweeps with no fresh mention this run, carried as lower-priority historical context.

best_practice_check.summary: The win levers now are multi-threading past a cooling single champion, manufacturing a forcing function against inertia, and converting the Sept Horizon invite into the first direct CPO conversation.
flags: Multi-thread through Amanda Moir, the warm AI-interested path to Helen Whitehouse, since single-thread Ludovic went quiet on 16 Jun 2026.
Build a cost-of-delay and Intake/ANA 'immediate impact' business case to give incoming Helen Whitehouse a reason to greenlight the paused POC.
Use the September Horizon invite (Amanda + Helen) as the dated exec touchpoint to secure the first direct CPO-office conversation.

Field read matches the calls: real curiosity (Ludovic's Vienna AI-event interest; Amanda's Agentic-AI questions) but no buyer-stated AI need inside the McCain deal and the AI dialogue went quiet after the CPO transition. Neither over- nor under-rated.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Shortlisted $150k Source-to-Pay deal frozen roughly 8 months behind McCain's procurement reorg; the only live thread is Amanda Moir's 25 Jun 2026 Agentic-AI interest, while champion Ludovic Verdelet has gone quiet and new CPO Helen Whitehouse, who must approve the restart, has never engaged.

win_position.text: Score holds mid-band because there is a real, sustained buyer relationship: Ludovic Verdelet carried four calls from Sep 2025 to Feb 2026 and pushed the POC internally ('let's jump on the POC, we still believe we have to go', 24 Oct 2025), and Amanda Moir gave strong Agentic-AI signals at the 25 Jun 2026 Toronto event.

It is held below par because Ludovic paused the POC himself on 24 Oct 2025 ('it has been accepted to put things on hold') and every restart target (Nov CPO approval, Jan POC, Feb org clarity, Mar reconnect) has since slipped.

Economic-buyer risk caps it further: new CPO Helen Whitehouse must re-approve the analytics strategy and has never spoken with Zycus, while champion Ludovic went non-responsive to email and WhatsApp per the 16 Jun 2026 Next Step.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is weak: the last recorded buyer call was 13 Feb 2026 and it was housekeeping only, with everything since being rep-initiated outreach or event logistics rather than a deal-advancing meeting.
One fresh spark: Amanda Moir attended the 25 Jun 2026 Toronto roundtable and asked substantive questions on Agentic AI, Intake/Orchestration and ANA (SF Task 26 Jun 2026), opening a second thread.
No customer-named dates and no commercial topics have surfaced since the Oct 2025 NDA discussion; the CPO reorg keeps resetting the clock.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is low: the buyer deliberately paused the initiative on 24 Oct 2025 and scoped procure-to-pay out on 10 Dec 2025, narrowing to an upstream analytics POC.
The NDA Ludovic agreed to on 24 Oct 2025 was never confirmed executed, so no data-sharing or POC has actually started.
Ludovic reaffirmed intent to move fast once the CPO approves ('every month left behind us without moving is a month lost', 10 Dec 2025).

deal_risk.text: The primary risk is do-nothing / organizational inertia: an approximately 8-month McCain procurement reorg has frozen the deal with no forcing function.
The deal is single-threaded on a champion who is himself uncertain of his post-reorg role ('will I decide to be part of it or not?', 13 Feb 2026) and has gone quiet.
The Best Case forecast is indefensible: no engaged economic buyer, no quantified business case, POC not started; the honest category is Pipeline.

deal_momentum.evidence: Last buyer call 13 Feb 2026 (housekeeping); since then only rep outreach and event logistics, no customer-named dates.

customer_commitment.evidence: Buyer paused the POC himself 24 Oct 2025 and scoped P2P out 10 Dec 2025; NDA never confirmed executed.

deal_risk.evidence: 8-month reorg freeze with no forcing function, single-threaded cooling champion, economic buyer never engaged.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay suite including procure-to-pay (eProcurement, eInvoicing)
scope_change.to: Upstream Source-to-Contract analytics POC (iAnalyze/ANA, Intake, tail spend); procure-to-pay explicitly out of scope
scope_change.detail: On 10 Dec 2025 the parties agreed to focus on the upstream 'S to C' components and to 'stay away from p to p' ('it's out of scope'), a defensive narrowing consistent with cost caution and a phased approach during the reorg.

critical_signals.text: The real threat is do-nothing: McCain paused the project itself during an roughly 8-month procurement reorg, not a rival vendor.
Amanda Moir, who reports to new CPO Helen Whitehouse, is newly engaged and AI-curious but the relationship is still nascent.
The strongest recent sign was Amanda Moir's 25 Jun questions on Agentic AI and Intake/ANA as immediate-impact areas; the 13 Feb call itself was only event logistics.
No pricing or ROI is on the table and the buyer scoped procure-to-pay out in December, shrinking the deal to an upstream analytics POC.

customer_preference.evidence: No decisive Zycus-specific next step is committed and the buyer paused the initiative himself; the only positive lean is Amanda Moir's Agentic-AI interest at the 25 Jun 2026 event.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative urgency only ('every month left behind us without moving is a month lost', 10 Dec 2025); no cost, savings or ROI figure quantified.

reasons.summary: Scope shrinking vs prior — On 10 Dec 2025 the parties agreed to focus on the upstream 'S to C' components and to 'stay away from p to p' ('it's out of scope'), a defensive narrowing consistent with cost caution and a phased app. Buyer likely getting defensive on cost or implementation/integration (phased over big-bang) — watch.

reasons.to: Upstream Source-to-Contract analytics POC (iAnalyze/ANA, Intake, tail spend); procure-to-pay explicitly out of scope

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — On 10 Dec 2025 the parties agreed to focus on the upstream 'S to C' components and to 'stay away from p to p' ('it's out of scope'), a defensive narrowing consi (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 43 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — On 10 Dec 2025 the parties agreed to focus on the upstream 'S to C' components and to 'stay away from p to p'….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 19 is anchored on the stage win (43), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: McCain Foods Limited — Shortlisted · $150K · closes 26 Feb
cro_panel.intro: Shortlisted $150k Source-to-Pay deal frozen roughly 8 months behind McCain's procurement reorg; the only live thread is Amanda Moir's 25 Jun 2026 Agentic-AI interest, while champion Ludovic Verdelet has gone quiet and new CPO Helen Whitehouse, who must approve the restart, has never engaged.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Score holds mid-band because there is a real, sustained buyer relationship: Ludovic Verdelet carried four calls from Sep 2025 to Feb 2026 and pushed the POC internally ('let's jump on the POC, we still believe we have to go', 24 Oct 2025), and Amanda Moir gave strong Agentic-AI signals at the 25 Jun 2026 Toronto event.

It is held below par because Ludovic paused the POC himself on 24 Oct 2025 ('it has been accepted to put things on hold') and every restart target (Nov CPO approval, Jan POC, Feb org clarity, Mar reconnect) has since slipped.

Economic-buyer risk caps it further: new CPO Helen Whitehouse must re-approve the analytics strategy and has never spoken with Zycus, while champion Ludovic went non-responsive to email and WhatsApp per the 16 Jun 2026 Next Step.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 43.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: Momentum is weak: the last recorded buyer call was 13 Feb 2026 and it was housekeeping only, with everything since being rep-initiated outreach or event logistics rather than a deal-advancing meeting.
One fresh spark: Amanda Moir attended the 25 Jun 2026 Toronto roundtable and asked substantive questions on Agentic AI, Intake/Orchestration and ANA (SF Task 26 Jun 2026), opening a second thread.
No customer-named dates and no commercial topics have surfaced since the Oct 2025 NDA discussion; the CPO reorg keeps resetting the clock.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

The deal is single-threaded on a champion who is himself uncertain of his post-reorg role ('will I decide to be part of it or not?', 13 Feb 2026) and has gone quiet.
The Best Case forecast is indefensible: no engaged economic buyer, no quantified business case, POC not started; the honest category is Pipeline.
